structured.name: Reset - Without Ongoing Transaction
structured.objective:
To enable the CSMS to request a Charging Station to reset itself or an EVSE, while there is no
ongoing transaction.
structured.description:
This use case covers how the CSMS can request the Charging Station to reset itself or an EVSE by
sending ResetRequest. (If ResetRequest contains an optional paramater evseId, then only a reset
of the specific EVSE is requested.) This could for example be necessary if the Charging Station is
not functioning correctly.
scenario: 1. The CSO requests the CSMS to reset the Charging Station or EVSE.
2. The CSMS sends ResetRequest requesting the Charging Station to reset itself or EVSE.
3. The CSMS requests for an OnIdle or Immediate reset.

4. The Charging Station responds with ResetResponse, indicating whether the Charging Station is
able to reset itself or EVSE.
5. The CSMS sends an optional notification to the CSO.

6. Only if no evseId was supplied, then after the reset, the Charging Station will proceed as in use
case B01.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station was able to reset itself or EVSE.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station not was able to reset itself or EVSE.
structured.remarks:
OnIdle refers to the "idle state" of a charging station. This is when the Charging Station is not
performing any use case related tasks that might interfere with a reset process. The most
obvious case is being involved in an active transaction, but there are other conditions when the
Charging Station is not idle, for example, when a firmware update process is ongoing, a log file is
uploaded to CSMS, a reservation is pending or a cable is still locked in the Charging Station.

requirements.precondition:
When the Charging Station receives a
ResetRequest.
requirements.definition: The Charging Station SHALL respond with a ResetResponse.

requirements.precondition: If the status was set to Inoperative by the CSMS.
requirements.definition:
After a reboot of the Charging Station, the EVSEs SHALL return
to the state Unavailable as prior to the reboot.

requirements.precondition:
B11.FR.01
AND no evseId parameter is supplied
AND
ResetResponse has status = Accepted.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY send a NotifyEventRequest for
component = "Connector", variable = "AvailabilityState",
actualValue = "Unavailable" and trigger = "Delta" for each EVSE,
and SHALL start a reboot.

The Charging Station SHALL proceed as described in use case
B01 - Cold Boot Charging Station.

requirements.precondition: If the status of an EVSE was Reserved.
requirements.definition:
After a reboot of the Charging Station or resetting of EVSE, the
EVSE(s) SHALL return to the state Reserved.

requirements.precondition:
B11.FR.01
AND
Charging Station is at this moment not able to
perform a reset.
requirements.definition: The Charging Station SHALL respond with a status = Rejected.

requirements.precondition:
B11.FR.01
AND
Charging Station cannot perform the reset now,
but has scheduled the reset for later
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a status =
Scheduled.

requirements.precondition:
B11.FR.01
AND an evseId parameter is supplied
AND
ResetResponse has status = Accepted.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY send a NotifyEventRequest for
component = "Connector", variable = "AvailabilityState",
actualValue = "Unavailable" and trigger = "Delta" for the EVSE,
and SHALL start a reset of the EVSE that is referred to by evseId
parameter.

requirements.precondition:
B11.FR.01
AND an evseId parameter is supplied
AND
Charging Station does not support resetting an
individual EVSE
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL return a ResetResponse with status
= Rejected

requirements.precondition:
When the Charging Station supports resetting of
an individual EVSE
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD set the device model variable
AllowReset to true for the EVSE.

chunks.content:
# B11 - Reset - Without Ongoing Transaction

**Objective:** To enable the CSMS to request a Charging Station to reset itself or an EVSE, while there is no
ongoing transaction.

**Description:** This use case covers how the CSMS can request the Charging Station to reset itself or an EVSE by
sending ResetRequest. (If ResetRequest contains an optional paramater evseId, then only a reset
of the specific EVSE is requested.) This could for example be necessary if the Charging Station is
not functioning correctly.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS, CSO

**Scenario:**
1. The CSO requests the CSMS to reset the Charging Station or EVSE.
2. The CSMS sends ResetRequest requesting the Charging Station to reset itself or EVSE.
3. The CSMS requests for an OnIdle or Immediate reset.
4. The Charging Station responds with ResetResponse, indicating whether the Charging Station is
able to reset itself or EVSE.
5. The CSMS sends an optional notification to the CSO.
6. Only if no evseId was supplied, then after the reset, the Charging Station will proceed as in use
case B01.

**Prerequisites:** No transaction is ongoing.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station was able to reset itself or EVSE.
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station not was able to reset itself or EVSE.

**Error handling:** n.a

**Remarks:** OnIdle refers to the "idle state" of a charging station. This is when the Charging Station is not
performing any use case related tasks that might interfere with a reset process. The most
obvious case is being involved in an active transaction, but there are other conditions when the
Charging Station is not idle, for example, when a firmware update process is ongoing, a log file is
uploaded to CSMS, a reservation is pending or a cable is still locked in the Charging Station.

# B11 - Requirements

**B11.FR.01**
Precondition: When the Charging Station receives a
ResetRequest.
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a ResetResponse.

**B11.FR.02**
Precondition: If the status was set to Inoperative by the CSMS.
After a reboot of the Charging Station, the EVSEs SHALL return
to the state Unavailable as prior to the reboot.

**B11.FR.03**
Precondition: B11.FR.01
AND no evseId parameter is supplied
AND
ResetResponse has status = Accepted.
The Charging Station MAY send a NotifyEventRequest for
component = "Connector", variable = "AvailabilityState",
actualValue = "Unavailable" and trigger = "Delta" for each EVSE,
and SHALL start a reboot.

**B11.FR.04**
Precondition: B11.FR.03
The Charging Station SHALL proceed as described in use case
B01 - Cold Boot Charging Station.

**B11.FR.05**
Precondition: If the status of an EVSE was Reserved.
After a reboot of the Charging Station or resetting of EVSE, the
EVSE(s) SHALL return to the state Reserved.

**B11.FR.06**
Precondition: B11.FR.01
AND
Charging Station is at this moment not able to
perform a reset.
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a status = Rejected.

**B11.FR.07**
Precondition: B11.FR.01
AND
Charging Station cannot perform the reset now,
but has scheduled the reset for later
The Charging Station SHALL respond with a status =
Scheduled.

**B11.FR.08**
Precondition: B11.FR.01
AND an evseId parameter is supplied
AND
ResetResponse has status = Accepted.
The Charging Station MAY send a NotifyEventRequest for
component = "Connector", variable = "AvailabilityState",
actualValue = "Unavailable" and trigger = "Delta" for the EVSE,
and SHALL start a reset of the EVSE that is referred to by evseId
parameter.

**B11.FR.09**
Precondition: B11.FR.01
AND an evseId parameter is supplied
AND
Charging Station does not support resetting an
individual EVSE
The Charging Station SHALL return a ResetResponse with status
= Rejected

**B11.FR.10**
Precondition: When the Charging Station supports resetting of
an individual EVSE
The Charging Station SHOULD set the device model variable
AllowReset to true for the EVSE.
